title: The Gate at the Scrub's Edge

features.name: the gate
features.desc-on-touch: iron in the shape of a question: hinges still turning, lock still mute after thirty years, the boundary itself uncertain whether it keeps something in or out

features.name: rust bloom
features.desc-on-touch: where oxidation has traced patterns into the metal surface, each bloom a slow conversation between iron and air, time written in colour

features.name: post remains
features.desc-on-touch: concrete pillars eroding into rubble, the gate still hung between what is leaving and what has already gone, a threshold no longer used

features.name: grown-through vine
features.desc-on-touch: vegetation pressing through the bars, the scrub reclaiming its boundary, green asserting that passage and prohibition have both rotted away

features.name: worn brass plaque
features.desc-on-touch: text ground smooth by rain and reading; you can guess what it said but not quite see it, the archive learning that forgetting is also preservation

inscription: the gate has oxidized into a question: passage or barrier, and does it matter after this long

file.name: Property_Survey_1962_Corroded_Gate_Section.pdf
file.note: original survey marking the boundary, now a document about forgetting, checksum: formally uncertain

exits.label: back into the scrub, toward the gymnasium
through the gate, toward the absent paddock where children played
along the fence line, where other boundaries have begun to rust
exits.to: NEW: the eastern fence run, where property claims are dissolving into eucalyptus
